home.title: David Herrera - IT Engineer & AI Solutions Architect
home.description: AI Solutions Architect and Software Engineer based in Mexico City. I design and ship systems built on LLMs, RAG, MCP, .NET, Python and Azure that deliver value in production.

projects.title: Projects - David Herrera
projects.description: Systems I've designed and shipped: enterprise RAG platforms, industrial software, Android RFID apps and automotive traceability.

work.title: Experience - David Herrera
work.description: Professional journey: from intern to AI Solutions Architect at BAACC Systems. LLMs, MCP, RAG, MLOps, .NET, Python, Azure.

education.title: Education - David Herrera
education.description: ICT Engineering (TESCo - TecNM), Programming Technician (CECyTEM) and continuous learning in AI, MLOps and development at Platzi. Azure AZ-900 and Python certifications.

contact.title: Contact - David Herrera
contact.description: Available for freelance projects, collaborations and full-time opportunities. Email, LinkedIn, GitHub and Resume (ES/EN) in PDF.

currently.label: currently building
currently.text: Connie v2 — letting any team query years of internal documents, databases and spreadsheets in plain language, and get cited answers in seconds.

work.description: The interesting part of making software isn't the tools — it's what happens once the system meets production and you have to keep it alive.

architect.title: AI Solutions Architect
achievements: Designed and shipped Connie from scratch (80% of the system): a production RAG platform serving 3 clients over their heterogeneous data —databases, PDFs, spreadsheets— with enterprise auth
End-to-end RAG pipelines on Azure: ingestion, chunking, retrieval, and serving with prompt observability
MCP servers connecting internal data sources to LLM clients — models consume tools without custom glue code
Brought MLOps practices to the team: retrieval evaluation, prompt and model versioning

fullstack.title: Fullstack Developer
achievements: Desktop apps in .NET and Python for the shop floor — 24/7 industrial operation
Internal web apps with .NET and DevExpress for operations workflows
Database design and administration on SQL Server and PostgreSQL
Android app with RFID reader for field inventory and traceability

junior.title: Junior Fullstack Developer
achievements: Database maintenance and technical documentation of the stack

webdev.title: Junior Web Developer
achievements: Website development and support with HTML, CSS, JavaScript and CMS (WordPress, Joomla, OpenCart)
Hosting, security certificates and domain administration

education.description: Academic background, certifications, and speaking engagements — because knowledge grows when shared.

sideCards.interestsBody: Generative AI, MLOps, distributed systems, specialty coffee, and — inevitably — typography.
sideCards.philosophyBody: «The best architecture is the one you don't need to defend.»

aboutSection.title: About me
aboutSection.heading: Software, data & AI applied to the real world.

bio.paragraph1: Software engineer building at the intersection of backend, data, and applied AI. Four years turning business problems into systems that ship, scale, and stay maintainable instead of rotting into tech debt.
bio.paragraph2: Comfortable across the stack — from data model to frontend — but my current focus is RAG, MCP, and MLOps architectures over corporate data. I care that the AI systems I design are operable, observable, and debuggable in real production.
bio.paragraph3: I give talks on AI and data science at universities —Tec de Monterrey, UPVM Tultitlán and TESCo— to audiences of ~200 people, closing the loop between academic theory and what actually happens once a system meets production.
